entities.name: Competitive dynamics in mature product categories
entities.context: Framework for understanding how competition plays out in established markets where incumbents face switching risk

entities.name: Market switching behavior
entities.context: Customer movement between competitors when satisfaction threshold drops; customers actively seek alternatives

entities.name: Satisfaction threshold
entities.context: Point at which customers become tired of incumbent and open to alternatives

entities.name: Switcher profile
entities.context: Customer segment identified as high-value early adopters who are dissatisfied with current solutions

entities.name: Relevance over volume
entities.context: Marketing principle that precision and message-market fit outperform broad reach in noisy markets

entities.name: Precision targeting
entities.context: Marketing approach favoring targeted, high-relevance campaigns over broad-based awareness

entities.name: Message-market fit
entities.context: Alignment between marketing message and specific audience needs; quality of audience over quantity

entities.name: Incumbent positioning gaps
entities.context: Strategic opportunity to emphasize what established competitors are missing

relationships.context: Switching becomes possible when satisfaction drops below threshold
Switchers are the customer segment most likely to change providers
Both principles emphasize quality and fit over broad reach
Targeted campaigns require strong message-market alignment
Positioning against gaps attracts dissatisfied switchers

relationships.from: Competitive dynamics in mature product categories
relationships.context: Mature market dynamics include customer switching patterns
